articles.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
articles.summary: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

articles.summary: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

articles.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
articles.summary: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

articles.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
articles.summary: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

articles.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
articles.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

articles.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
articles.summary: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

articles.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
articles.summary: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

articles.title: US reportedly considering sweeping new chip export controls
articles.summary: In an alleged drafted proposal, the U.S. government would play a role in every chip export sale regardless of which country it's coming from.

articles.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
articles.summary: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

articles.title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
articles.summary: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

articles.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it an
